text: This matter concerns an appeal from a Texas Service Center decision in USCIS case number 25674629. The Petitioner is a physician-researcher in urologic oncology who seeks employment-based second preference immigrant classification and a national interest waiver of the job-offer requirement. The Director denied the petition, and the matter is before the Administrative Appeals Office on appeal. On appeal, the Petitioner submitted additional evidence and a brief asserting eligibility for a national interest waiver. He stated that he will continue providing clinical care to cancer patients, conduct research, lead clinical trials, work to reduce healthcare costs, and develop innovative surgical approaches to improve care for patients with cancer. In support, he submitted materials from the American Cancer Society and the National Cancer Institute concerning urologic cancers and the economic burdens of cancer care, along with expert letters discussing the implications of his research for patient treatment and recovery. The record also includes his curriculum vitae, academic records, two published articles, evidence of peer review activity, articles citing his research findings, and letters of endorsement from experts in senior academic and medical positions describing his research accomplishments, including development of surgical techniques that significantly affect patient recovery and have been used by medical centers in the United States and abroad. The record further contains evidence described as showing that he is a research physician with influential published research and surgical techniques and treatment methods adopted by others, as well as evidence described as showing broader economic and public-health benefits associated with progress in improving treatment methods for cancer and other urologic disorders.
statutes:
To establish eligibility for a national interest waiver, a petitioner must first demonstrate qualification for the underlying EB-2 visa classification, as either an advanced degree professional or an individual of exceptional ability in the sciences, arts, or business. Section 203(b )(2)(B)(i) of the Act. If a petitioner demonstrates eligibility for the underlying EB-2 classification, they must then establish that they merit a discretionary waiver of the job offer requirement "in the national interest." Section 203(b )(2)(B)(i) of the Act. While neither the statute nor the pertinent regulations define the term "national interest," Matter of Dhanasar, 26 I&N Dec . 884, 889 (AAO 2016), provides the framework for adjudicating national interest waiver petitions. Dhanasar states that U.S . Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) may , as matter of discretion 1, grant a national interest waiver if the petitioner demonstrates that: 1 See also Poursina v. USCIS , 936 F.3d 868 (9th Cir. 2019) (finding USCIS' decision to grant or deny a national interest waiver to be discretionary in nature).

• The proposed endeavor has both substantial merit and national importance; • The individual is well-positioned to advance their proposed endeavor; and • On balance, waiving the job offer requirement would benefit the United States.
question: Should this case be accepted or dismissed?
label: Accepted
reference_prolog:
% ----------------------------
% Facts from the case record
% ----------------------------

petitioner_is_physician_researcher.
specialty_urologic_oncology.

filed_form_i140.
seeks_eb2.
seeks_national_interest_waiver.

% Employment / proposed endeavor
employed_as_resident_physician_in_urology_at_university_at_filing.
anticipated_continuing_through_fellowship.
subsequently_began_urologic_oncology_fellowship_at_different_institution.

% Education
has_doctor_of_medicine_degree.
md_awarded_2017.

% Proposed work description (endeavor)
proposed_endeavor_clinical_care_for_cancer_patients.
proposed_endeavor_conduct_studies_and_data_analysis.
proposed_endeavor_lead_clinical_trials.
proposed_endeavor_develop_innovative_surgical_approaches.
endeavor_addresses_significant_morbidity_and_mortality.

% Evidence submitted
submitted_cv.
submitted_academic_records.
submitted_two_published_articles.
evidence_of_peer_review_activity.
evidence_research_cited_by_others.
documentation_of_conference_presentations.

submitted_background_information_on_urologic_cancers.
submitted_background_information_on_economic_burden_of_cancer_care.

submitted_expert_endorsement_letters.

% Endorsement letter highlights (as asserted evidence)
letters_describe_development_of_surgical_techniques_used_in_us_and_abroad.
letters_describe_publications_in_peer_reviewed_journals.
letters_describe_authorship_of_aua_chapter.
letters_describe_authorship_of_aua_cme_test.
letters_describe_presentations_at_seminars.
letters_describe_three_projects_included_at_aua_annual_conference.
letters_describe_patient_education_study_on_over_the_counter_supplements.

% Procedural posture
director_denied_petition.
appeal_filed.
appeal_seeks_reversal.
additional_evidence_submitted_on_appeal.
brief_submitted_asserting_niw_eligibility.

% ----------------------------
% Legal framework encoded
% ----------------------------

% EB-2 threshold: advanced degree professional (here: MD).
eb2_advanced_degree_professional :-
    has_doctor_of_medicine_degree.

% ----------------------------
% Dhanasar prongs
% ----------------------------

% Prong 1: substantial merit and national importance.
% Substantial merit is supported by cancer care/research and morbidity/mortality.
substantial_merit :-
    proposed_endeavor_clinical_care_for_cancer_patients,
    (   proposed_endeavor_conduct_studies_and_data_analysis
    ;   proposed_endeavor_lead_clinical_trials
    ;   proposed_endeavor_develop_innovative_surgical_approaches
    ),
    endeavor_addresses_significant_morbidity_and_mortality.

% National importance: record contains general cancer burden info, but no specific
% showing that THIS petitioner’s proposed work has national-level impact beyond
% his local employment context. We therefore do not conclude national importance.
national_importance :-
    fail.

prong1_substantial_merit_and_national_importance :-
    substantial_merit,
    national_importance.

% Prong 2: well-positioned to advance the endeavor.
% Supported by credentials and record of publications, citations, peer review, presentations, endorsements.
well_positioned :-
    eb2_advanced_degree_professional,
    submitted_two_published_articles,
    evidence_of_peer_review_activity,
    (   evidence_research_cited_by_others
    ;   documentation_of_conference_presentations
    ),
    submitted_expert_endorsement_letters.

% Prong 3: on balance, waiver benefits the United States.
% Requires weighing; without prong 1 national importance, the balance is not met here.
waiver_benefits_united_states_on_balance :-
    fail.

% ----------------------------
% Overall eligibility
% ----------------------------

eligibility_met :-
    eb2_advanced_degree_professional,
    prong1_substantial_merit_and_national_importance,
    well_positioned,
    waiver_benefits_united_states_on_balance.

% ----------------------------
% Required binary output
% ----------------------------

decision(Result) :-
    (   eligibility_met
    ->  Result = 'Accepted'
    ;   Result = 'Dismissed'
    ).

run :-
    catch(
        (   decision(Result),
            writeln(Result)
        ),
        error(existence_error(procedure, PI), _),
        handle_undefined(PI)
    ).

handle_undefined(Name/Arity) :-
    (   current_predicate(Name/OtherArity),
        OtherArity \= Arity
    ->  format('Programming error: called ~w/~w, but only ~w/~w is defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity, Name, OtherArity])
    ;   format('Lack of information: predicate ~w/~w is not defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity])
    ).

text: The Petitioner is a technology company involved in electrical product and integrated software and hardware development and production. It seeks EB-2 classification for the Beneficiary, an embedded systems architect who is described as a member of the professions holding an advanced degree, and it also seeks a national interest waiver of the job offer requirement. The Director of the Texas Service Center initially denied the petition and later affirmed that decision on motion, concluding that the record did not establish that a waiver of the job offer requirement would be in the national interest. The Petitioner initially requested withdrawal of the appeal, and USCIS later reopened the appeal sua sponte to consider the merits of the Petitioner's appeal brief. The matter is before the Administrative Appeals Office on appeal. In support of the request, the record includes letters of support discussing how the Beneficiary's undertaking contributes to advancements in improved renewable energy technologies. The Petitioner submitted documentation indicating that circuit breaker and EV charging technology the Beneficiary has been essential in developing has been implemented with commercial and industrial clients throughout the United States and disseminated to others in the field through publication and presentation of his work. The Petitioner also represented that it intends to have the Beneficiary continue research and development in motor controls for large industrial and commercial facilities and EV charging, later stating that his work would focus on commercial fleet and multifamily EV charging, residential demand response, and solar energy storage product lines, and that this work would be shared through scientific conferences and meetings. Additional submitted information included an article about U.S. government initiatives to build out a national EV charging network to expand charging accessibility and support the competitiveness of the U.S. EV manufacturing industry. The record also contains material indicating widespread benefits associated with research progress in next-generation circuit breaker and EV charging technology.
statutes:
To establish eligibility for a national interest waiver, a petitioner must first demonstrate the beneficiary's qualification for the underlying EB-2 visa classification, as either an advanced degree professional or an individual of exceptional ability in the sciences, arts, or business. Section 203(b )(2)(B)(i) of the Act. An advanced degree is any United States academic or professional degree or a foreign equivalent degree above that of a bachelor's degree. A United States bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent degree followed by five years of progressive experience in the specialty is the equivalent of a master's degree. 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(k)(2). 1 The Petitioner was permitted a period of 30 days in which to provide a supplemental brief. We did not receive a supplemental submission within that time frame and therefore consider the record to be complete as presently constituted.

If a petitioner demonstrates the beneficiary's eligibility for the underlying EB-2 classification, it must then establish that they merit a discretionary waiver of the job offer requirement "in the national interest." Section 203(b )(2)(B)(i) of the Act. While neither the statute nor the pertinent regulations define the term "national interest," Matter ofDhanasar, 26 I&N Dec. 884, 889 (AAO 2016), provides the framework for adjudicating national interest waiver petitions. Dhanasar states that U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) may, as matter of discretion, 2 grant a national interest waiver if the petitioner demonstrates that: • The proposed endeavor has both substantial merit and national importance; • The individual is well-positioned to advance their proposed endeavor; and • On balance, waiving the job offer requirement would benefit the United States. 3
question: Should this case be accepted or dismissed?
label: Accepted
reference_prolog:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Facts from the case
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

petitioner_is_technology_company.
petitioner_develops_and_produces_electrical_products_and_integrated_software_hardware.

beneficiary_is_embedded_systems_architect.
beneficiary_has_masters_degree_in_electrical_engineering.
masters_degree_year_2015.

petition_type_eb2.
niw_requested.

employed_by_petitioner_at_filing.

% Proposed endeavor / duties
duties_include_research_and_develop_firmware_for_proprietary_electrical_product.
duties_include_develop_web_based_system_used_in_products_and_ev_charging_stations.

proposed_focus_motor_controls_for_large_industrial_and_commercial_facilities.
proposed_focus_ev_charging.
proposed_focus_commercial_fleet_multifamily_ev_charging.
proposed_focus_residential_demand_response.
proposed_focus_solar_energy_storage_product_lines.
intends_to_share_work_through_conferences_and_meetings.

% Evidence submitted
submitted_academic_credentials.
submitted_documentation_past_successes_circuit_breaker_and_ev_charging_technology.
evidence_technology_implemented_with_commercial_and_industrial_clients_across_us.
submitted_market_and_research_information.
submitted_letters_of_support.
letters_discuss_contributions_to_renewable_energy_technologies.
citations_to_nstc_clean_energy_generation_and_storage.

% Procedural posture
director_denied.
director_affirmed_on_motion.
petitioner_appealed.
initial_request_withdraw_appeal_then_claimed_erroneous.

% Record note: no supplemental brief received (record complete)
no_supplemental_brief_received.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Legal framework (Dhanasar) encoded as rules
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% EB-2 qualification: advanced degree professional (only this path modeled here)
advanced_degree :-
    beneficiary_has_masters_degree_in_electrical_engineering.

eb2_qualification_met :-
    petition_type_eb2,
    advanced_degree.

% Prong 1: substantial merit and national importance
% Substantial merit: clean energy / EV charging / storage / demand response is meritorious.
substantial_merit :-
    (   proposed_focus_ev_charging
    ;   proposed_focus_solar_energy_storage_product_lines
    ;   proposed_focus_residential_demand_response
    ;   citations_to_nstc_clean_energy_generation_and_storage
    ).

% National importance requires evidence beyond internal employer benefit; here we only have
% implementation with clients and general market info, but no specific showing of broader
% national impact attributable to the beneficiary's proposed endeavor.
national_importance :-
    fail.

prong1_met :-
    substantial_merit,
    national_importance.

% Prong 2: well-positioned to advance the endeavor
well_positioned :-
    beneficiary_is_embedded_systems_architect,
    submitted_academic_credentials,
    submitted_documentation_past_successes_circuit_breaker_and_ev_charging_technology,
    submitted_letters_of_support.

prong2_met :-
    well_positioned.

% Prong 3: on balance, waiver benefits the United States
% Requires prongs 1 and 2 as foundation and additional balancing; not met if prong1 fails.
prong3_met :-
    prong1_met,
    prong2_met,
    fail.

% Overall NIW eligibility
niw_eligibility_met :-
    prong1_met,
    prong2_met,
    prong3_met.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Final eligibility condition
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

eligibility_met :-
    eb2_qualification_met,
    niw_eligibility_met.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Entrypoint (required structure)
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

decision(Result) :-
    (   eligibility_met
    ->  Result = 'Accepted'
    ;   Result = 'Dismissed'
    ).

run :-
    catch(
        (   decision(Result),
            writeln(Result)
        ),
        error(existence_error(procedure, PI), _),
        handle_undefined(PI)
    ).

handle_undefined(Name/Arity) :-
    (   current_predicate(Name/OtherArity),
        OtherArity \= Arity
    ->  format('Programming error: called ~w/~w, but only ~w/~w is defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity, Name, OtherArity])
    ;   format('Lack of information: predicate ~w/~w is not defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity])
    ).

text: The Petitioner seeks immigrant investor classification, and the matter is before the Administrative Appeals Office on appeal. In her petition, she indicated that she had invested $500,000 in the new commercial enterprise since November 21, 2018, and that she owned 13.07 percent of the enterprise while 16 other individuals also held ownership interests. According to the enterprise's business plan, it intended to own and operate a new II Suites hotel in Pennsylvania. The plan estimated total project costs of $10.9 million, to be funded by bank loans of $7,215,000, developer equity of $3,190,044, and EB-5 equity of $500,000. The Chief issued a request for evidence and a notice of intent to deny seeking evidence of the lawful sources of investment capital from all investors, including non-EB-5 investors. In response, the Petitioner submitted a chart indicating that from March 1, 2018 to November 26, 2019, the Petitioner, 13 other individuals, and two entities invested a total of $2,482,000 in the enterprise. She also submitted copies of government-issued identification documents for 19 individuals, company formation or verification documents for two entity investors, affidavits from 19 individuals stating that their investments were obtained through lawful means and not acquired directly or indirectly through unlawful means, and bank statements showing outgoing wire transfers or cancelled checks from those individuals' accounts. On appeal, the Petitioner argues that she needed to show only that the non-EB-5 investors' funds were not unlawfully obtained, that the source-of-funds evidentiary requirements apply to her own invested capital rather than requiring her to trace the ultimate source of other investors' funds, and that funds moving through the U.S. banking system and associated with a Small Business Administration loan should be treated as lawfully sourced.
statutes:
The regulation at 8 C.F.R. § 204.6(g)(l) provides that a new commercial enterprise may be used as the basis for a Form 1-526 even though there are several owners of the enterprise as long as the source(s) of all capital investment is identified, and all invested capital has been derived by lawful means. 8 C.F.R. § 204.6 states: (g) Multiple investors - (1) Ge neral. Th e establishm ent of a new commercia l enterprise ma y be used as the basis of a petition for classification as an alien entrepreneur by more than one investor, provided each petitioning investor has invested or is actively in the

process of investing the required amount for the area in w hich the new commercial enterprise is principally doing business, and provided each individual investment results in the creation ofat least ten full-time positions for qualifying employees. T he establishm ent ofanew commercial enterprise ma y be used as the basis ofa petition for classification as an alien entrepreneur even though there are several owne rs of the enterprise, including persons w ho are not seeking classification under section 203(b )(5) of the Act and non­ natural persons, both foreign and dom estic, provided that the source(s) of all capital invested is identified and all invested capital has been derived by law ful mean s. (j) Initial evidence to accomp any petition. A petition submitted for classification as an alien entrepreneur must be accompanied by evidence that the alien has invested or is actively in the process of investing lawfully obtained capital in a new commercial enterprise in the United States, which will create full-time positions for not fewer than 10 qualifying employees. (3) To show that the petitioner has invested, investing, capital obtained through lawful accompanied, as applicable, by: (i) Foreign business registration records; or is actively in the process of means, the petition must be (ii) Corporate, partnership ( or any other entity in any form which has filed in any country or subdivision thereof any return described in this subpart), and personal tax returns including income, franchise, property (whether real, personal, or intangible), or any other tax returns of any kind filed within five years, with any taxing jurisdiction in or outside the United States by or on behalf of the petitioner; (iii) Evidence identifying any other source(s) of capital; or (iv) Certified copies of any judgments or evidence of all pending governmental civil or criminal actions, governmental administrative proceedings, and any private civil actions (pending or otherwise) involving monetary judgments against the petitioner from any court in or outside the United States within the past fifteen years.
question: Should this case be accepted or dismissed?
label: Accepted
reference_prolog:
% =========================
% AAO Case Parser (EB-5)
% =========================

% -------------------------
% Core facts from the record
% -------------------------

case_type(eb5_i526).
filed_year_2019.

petitioner_is_foreign_national.
petitioner_claimed_investment_amount_500k.
petitioner_claimed_investment_start_date('2018-11-21').
petitioner_claimed_tea.
petitioner_claimed_ownership_percent_13_07.

nce_has_multiple_owners.
nce_has_16_other_individual_coowners.
nce_has_two_entity_investors.
nce_total_project_cost_about_10_9_million.
nce_funding_sources(bank_loans).
nce_funding_sources(developer_equity).
nce_funding_sources(eb5_equity).

agency_issued_rfe.
agency_issued_noid.

record_contains_chart_of_investment_dates_and_amounts.
record_shows_other_investors_contributed_total_2_482_million_between_2018_03_01_and_2019_11_26.

submitted_govt_ids_for_19_individuals.
submitted_formation_docs_for_entity_investors.
submitted_bank_statements_showing_outgoing_wires_or_checks_for_non_eb5_investors.
submitted_affidavits_from_19_coowners_claiming_lawful_source_and_no_criminal_activity.

denied_by_ipo_chief.
denial_ground_failure_to_establish_all_invested_capital_lawful.

appeal_filed.
appeal_arguments_only_petitioner_funds_needed.
appeal_arguments_no_need_trace_non_eb5_ultimate_sources.
appeal_arguments_us_banking_presumption.
appeal_arguments_sba_loan_presumption.

% ---------------------------------------
% Governing rule distilled from 8 C.F.R.
% ---------------------------------------

% For an NCE with multiple owners (including non-EB-5 investors),
% the petitioner must identify the sources of ALL capital invested
% and establish that ALL invested capital was derived by lawful means.
regulation_requires_all_invested_capital_lawful_for_multiple_investor_nce :-
    nce_has_multiple_owners.

% Initial evidence concept (not exhaustively modeled): requires evidence
% that invested capital is lawfully obtained; regulation also allows USCIS
% to require evidence identifying other sources of capital.
regulation_allows_request_for_other_sources_evidence :-
    agency_issued_rfe.

% ---------------------------------------
% Legal tests / decision predicates
% ---------------------------------------

% In this case, "lawful means" for ALL invested capital is not established
% merely by: (a) IDs, (b) bank outgoing transfers, or (c) affidavits
% that funds are lawful, without underlying source documentation.
affidavits_and_outgoing_transfers_without_underlying_sources_are_insufficient.

% The record does NOT show that the ultimate lawful sources for ALL
% non-EB-5 investors were documented with tax returns, business records,
% or other probative source tracing evidence.
ultimate_sources_for_all_investors_documented :-
    % This would be true only if the record included complete lawful-source tracing
    % for all invested capital (including non-EB-5 investors). Not present here.
    fail.

all_invested_capital_lawful :-
    ultimate_sources_for_all_investors_documented.

sources_of_all_capital_identified :-
    ultimate_sources_for_all_investors_documented.

% Petitioner’s appellate theories do not negate the regulatory requirement.
appeal_theories_do_not_override_regulation :-
    regulation_requires_all_invested_capital_lawful_for_multiple_investor_nce,
    ( appeal_arguments_only_petitioner_funds_needed
    ; appeal_arguments_no_need_trace_non_eb5_ultimate_sources
    ; appeal_arguments_us_banking_presumption
    ; appeal_arguments_sba_loan_presumption
    ).

% Eligibility is met only if, for a multiple-investor NCE,
% sources of all capital are identified and all invested capital is lawful.
eligibility_met :-
    regulation_requires_all_invested_capital_lawful_for_multiple_investor_nce,
    sources_of_all_capital_identified,
    all_invested_capital_lawful,
    \+ appeal_theories_do_not_override_regulation.

% -------------------------
% Entrypoint (required)
% -------------------------
decision(Result) :-
    (   eligibility_met
    ->  Result = 'Accepted'
    ;   Result = 'Dismissed'
    ).

run :-
    catch(
        (   decision(Result),
            writeln(Result)
        ),
        error(existence_error(procedure, PI), _),
        handle_undefined(PI)
    ).

handle_undefined(Name/Arity) :-
    (   current_predicate(Name/OtherArity),
        OtherArity \= Arity
    ->  format('Programming error: called ~w/~w, but only ~w/~w is defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity, Name, OtherArity])
    ;   format('Lack of information: predicate ~w/~w is not defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity])
    ).

text: This matter concerns an appeal from a Nebraska Service Center decision on a Form I-601 application to waive inadmissibility grounds. The applicant applied for an immigrant visa abroad and sought a waiver after being found inadmissible on a human-smuggling ground. The Department of State determined that he was inadmissible because he had knowingly aided several noncitizens to enter the United States in violation of law, and the Nebraska Service Center director denied the waiver application on the stated ground that those individuals were not the applicant's spouse, parent, son, or daughter. On appeal, the applicant states that he did not knowingly assist those noncitizens to enter unlawfully and therefore does not require a waiver. The record further states that, after the director's decision, the Department of State determined that the applicant was not inadmissible on that ground.
statutes: Any noncitizen who at any time knowingly has encouraged, induced, assisted, abetted, or aided any othernoncitizen to enteror to try to enter the United States in violation oflaw is inadmissible. Section 212( a)( 6)(E)(i) of the Act. A discretionary waiver is available for humanitarian purposes, to assure family unity, or when it is otherwise in the public interest in the case of an applicant seeking admission or adjustment of status as an immediate relative or immigrant under section 203(a) (other than paragraph ( 4) thereof), if the applicant has encouraged, induced, assisted, abetted, or aided only an individual who at the time of the offense was the applicant's spouse, parent, son, or daughter (and no other individual) to enter the United States in violation of law. Section 212( d)(l 1) of the Act.
question: Should this case be accepted or dismissed?
label: Dismissed
reference_prolog:
/* Facts from the case */

petitioner_resides_abroad.
applied_for_immigrant_visa.

consular_officer_found_inadmissible_for_human_smuggling.
consular_officer_determined_knowingly_aided_several_noncitizens_unlawful_entry.

filed_form_i_601_waiver_request.

director_denied_waiver.
director_relied_on_consular_inadmissibility_finding.
director_concluded_not_eligible_for_waiver_because_not_qualifying_relatives_only.

on_appeal_petitioner_claims_no_knowing_assistance.
on_appeal_petitioner_claims_no_waiver_required.

/* Legal rules modeled */

/* INA 212(a)(6)(E)(i): inadmissible if knowingly assisted another noncitizen to enter unlawfully. */
inadmissible_for_smuggling :-
    consular_officer_found_inadmissible_for_human_smuggling,
    consular_officer_determined_knowingly_aided_several_noncitizens_unlawful_entry.

/* INA 212(d)(11): waiver only if assistance was ONLY to spouse/parent/son/daughter (and no other individual). */
waiver_available_under_212d11 :-
    helped_only_qualifying_relative,
    no_other_individual_helped.

/* Case-specific finding stated by director: the people allegedly assisted were not spouse/parent/son/daughter. */
helped_only_qualifying_relative :-
    fail.

no_other_individual_helped :-
    fail.

/* Eligibility: either not inadmissible, or inadmissible but eligible for the waiver sought. */
eligibility_met :-
    (   \+ inadmissible_for_smuggling
    ;   ( inadmissible_for_smuggling,
          waiver_available_under_212d11
        )
    ).

decision(Result) :-
    (   eligibility_met
    ->  Result = 'Accepted'
    ;   Result = 'Dismissed'
    ).

run :-
    catch(
        (   decision(Result),
            writeln(Result)
        ),
        error(existence_error(procedure, PI), _),
        handle_undefined(PI)
    ).

handle_undefined(Name/Arity) :-
    (   current_predicate(Name/OtherArity),
        OtherArity \= Arity
    ->  format('Programming error: called ~w/~w, but only ~w/~w is defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity, Name, OtherArity])
    ;   format('Lack of information: predicate ~w/~w is not defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity])
    ).

text: The Applicant is a 33-year-old transgender native and citizen of Mexico who was granted U-1 nonimmigrant status from October 2015 to September 2019 and timely filed an application for lawful permanent resident status in March 2019 based on that status. The Director of the Vermont Center denied the application, the Applicant's subsequent appeal was dismissed, and the matter is now before the Administrative Appeals Office on a motion to reopen and reconsider. In the prior appeal decision, the Office described positive and mitigating material in the record, including evidence of the Applicant's victimization and related trauma, her assistance to law enforcement in a domestic violence investigation, family ties in the United States including her mother and lawful permanent resident sister, her residence in the United States, her fear of violence in Mexico because she is transgender, her employment, letters and affidavits of support, and community involvement through church attendance. The record also reflects convictions for Public Fighting in 2007, False Information in 2007, Prostitution in 2007, Prostitution in 2012, and Operating While Intoxicated in 2018. The arrest report for the 2018 incident states that her blood alcohol content was almost double the legal limit, that she failed several field sobriety tests, that she caused property damage to another vehicle, that she urinated and defecated herself, and that there were open alcohol containers in her car. On motion, the Applicant submitted additional materials including a new affidavit, an affidavit from the person who was with her during the 2018 arrest, a certificate of completion for a court-mandated 48-hour operating-while-intoxicated program, a psychologist's letter stating that she did not need substance abuse treatment, a certificate for a motor vehicle breathalyzer device, a receipt for an operating-while-intoxicated continuing education course, and other documentation. In affidavits submitted on appeal and on motion, the Applicant states that she apologized for her criminal behavior and has made positive changes in her life. In a newly submitted affidavit, she states that she drove during the 2018 incident to help a friend who potentially had alcohol poisoning. On motion, she argues that the Office erred in its discretionary evaluation, did not adequately account for her positive equities and rehabilitation evidence, placed too much weight on a single 2018 operating-while-intoxicated conviction, gave excessive weight to the underlying police report, and that her newly submitted evidence supports reopening or reconsideration.
statutes: A motion to reopen must state new facts to be proved and be supported by affidavits or other evidence. 8 C.F.R. § 103.5(a)(2). A motion to reconsider must establish that our decision was based on an incorrect application of the law or U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) policy and that the decision was incorrect based on the evidence in the record as the time of the initial decision. 8 C .F.R. § 103.5(a)(3). The burden of proof is on the applicant to demonstrate eligibility by a preponderance of the evidence. Section 291 of the Act, 8 U.S.C. § 1361; MatterofChawathe, 25 I&N Dec. 369, 375 (AAO 2010).
question: Should this case be accepted or dismissed?
label: Dismissed
reference_prolog:
% -----------------------------
% Facts from the record
% -----------------------------

% Identity/background
petitioner_transgender.
petitioner_native_mexico.
petitioner_citizen_mexico.
petitioner_age_33.

% U-1 status and adjustment filing
granted_u1_status_oct2015_sep2019.
timely_filed_i485_march2019.

% Victim assistance / positive equities
victim_of_domestic_violence.
assisted_law_enforcement.
submitted_affidavits_and_support_letters.

family_in_us.
mother_in_us.
lpr_sister_in_us.
maintained_employment.
attends_church.
participated_in_aa.
fear_of_violence_in_mexico_due_to_transgender.

% Criminal history
conviction_public_fighting_2007.
conviction_false_information_2007.
conviction_prostitution_2007.
conviction_prostitution_2012.
conviction_owi_2018.

% OWI arrest report aggravating facts
owi_bac_nearly_double_limit.
failed_field_sobriety_tests.
struck_another_vehicle_property_damage.
open_alcohol_containers_in_car.
urinated_and_defecated_self.

% Post-conviction compliance / claimed rehabilitation
completed_probationary_requirements.
completed_owi_first_time_offender_program.
submitted_rehabilitation_evidence.

% Motion arguments/material
motion_claimed_discretion_error.
argued_positive_equities_ignored.
argued_remorse.
argued_waived_convictions_should_not_be_reconsidered.
argued_single_owi_overweighted.
argued_police_report_overweighted.
argued_drove_to_help_friend_possible_alcohol_poisoning.
argued_constitutional_violation.

% New evidence submitted on motion
new_personal_affidavit_submitted.
affidavit_from_companion_submitted.
certificate_48hr_owi_program_submitted.
psychologist_letter_no_substance_abuse_treatment_needed.
breathalyzer_device_certificate_submitted.
owi_continuing_education_receipt_submitted.
other_documentation_submitted.

% -----------------------------
% Legal framework (modeled)
% -----------------------------

% A motion to reopen requires new facts supported by evidence.
motion_to_reopen_requirements_met :-
    new_facts_present,
    supported_by_affidavits_or_other_evidence.

new_facts_present :-
    new_personal_affidavit_submitted;
    affidavit_from_companion_submitted;
    certificate_48hr_owi_program_submitted;
    psychologist_letter_no_substance_abuse_treatment_needed;
    breathalyzer_device_certificate_submitted;
    owi_continuing_education_receipt_submitted;
    other_documentation_submitted.

supported_by_affidavits_or_other_evidence :-
    new_personal_affidavit_submitted;
    affidavit_from_companion_submitted;
    certificate_48hr_owi_program_submitted;
    psychologist_letter_no_substance_abuse_treatment_needed;
    breathalyzer_device_certificate_submitted;
    owi_continuing_education_receipt_submitted;
    other_documentation_submitted.

% A motion to reconsider must show incorrect application of law/policy and
% that the decision was incorrect based on the record at the time.
% Here, the record does not establish such legal error as a modeled fact.
motion_to_reconsider_requirements_met :-
    incorrect_application_of_law_or_policy,
    decision_incorrect_on_prior_record.

% Not established in provided facts (left intentionally without clauses):
% incorrect_application_of_law_or_policy.
% decision_incorrect_on_prior_record.

% Discretion analysis model:
% Serious negative factors weigh against favorable discretion.
serious_negative_factors_present :-
    conviction_owi_2018,
    (   owi_bac_nearly_double_limit
    ;   struck_another_vehicle_property_damage
    ;   open_alcohol_containers_in_car
    ;   failed_field_sobriety_tests
    ),
    multiple_prior_convictions_present.

multiple_prior_convictions_present :-
    conviction_public_fighting_2007,
    conviction_false_information_2007,
    conviction_prostitution_2007,
    conviction_prostitution_2012.

positive_equities_present :-
    victim_of_domestic_violence,
    assisted_law_enforcement,
    family_in_us,
    maintained_employment.

rehabilitation_evidence_present :-
    completed_probationary_requirements,
    completed_owi_first_time_offender_program,
    (   participated_in_aa
    ;   psychologist_letter_no_substance_abuse_treatment_needed
    ;   certificate_48hr_owi_program_submitted
    ;   owi_continuing_education_receipt_submitted
    ;   breathalyzer_device_certificate_submitted
    ).

% Modeled outcome rule:
% To be accepted on motion, petitioner must at least meet reopen or reconsider standards,
% and must show eligibility met (including meriting discretion).
% Given serious negative factors, eligibility is not met unless overcome.
merits_favorable_discretion :-
    positive_equities_present,
    rehabilitation_evidence_present,
    \+ serious_negative_factors_present.

% Eligibility in this motion context:
% Accept only if motion requirements are met AND discretion is favorable.
eligibility_met :-
    (   motion_to_reopen_requirements_met
    ;   motion_to_reconsider_requirements_met
    ),
    merits_favorable_discretion.

% -----------------------------
% Entrypoint (required structure)
% -----------------------------
decision(Result) :-
    (   eligibility_met
    ->  Result = 'Accepted'
    ;   Result = 'Dismissed'
    ).

run :-
    catch(
        (   decision(Result),
            writeln(Result)
        ),
        error(existence_error(procedure, PI), _),
        handle_undefined(PI)
    ).

handle_undefined(Name/Arity) :-
    (   current_predicate(Name/OtherArity),
        OtherArity \= Arity
    ->  format('Programming error: called ~w/~w, but only ~w/~w is defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity, Name, OtherArity])
    ;   format('Lack of information: predicate ~w/~w is not defined.~n',
               [Name, Arity])
    ).
